Description: As an Admin, I want to manage student profiles from onboarding through the full lifecycle so that the centre keeps clean and reliable student records.
As an Admin, I want to record offline tuition payments so that student package balances are always up-to-date.
As an Admin, I want to set up and manage the teaching timetable, preventing double-bookings, so that academic resources are allocated efficiently.
As an Admin, I want to review and act on package renewal alerts so that I can proactively retain students before their sessions run out.
As a Teacher, I want to view my personalised timetable and record accurate class attendance so that daily teaching operations run smoothly.
As a Teacher, I want to submit academic performance feedback within the required SLA window so that the centre maintains evaluation standards.
As the System, I want to run nightly depletion scans and dispatch zero-touch parent notifications automatically so that manual workload is minimised.
As a Stakeholder, I want the system to meet defined quality, security and performance standards so that the product is production-ready.
As a Developer, I want the system infrastructure (Supabase, hosting, CI/CD) set up and configured so that all other epics can be built and deployed reliably.
As an Academic Admin, I want to create a new student profile (with parent contact info and initial package) so that the student is enrolled and can begin attending classes.

Summary: API: POST /api/students — create student & parent record
Description: Implement POST /api/students endpoint. Validates payload (Full Name, Parent Email, Parent Phone mandatory). Creates Student + Parent rows in DB. Writes Activity_Log entry. Returns 201 with student ID.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a valid JSON payload; When called with Admin JWT; Then 201 response with created student object.

Summary: API: POST /api/student-packages — assign initial package to student
Description: Implement endpoint to link a Package record to a newly created Student, initialising remaining_sessions from the Package.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a valid student_id and package_id; When called; Then student_package row created with correct session count.

Summary: UI: Add New Student Profile Screen
Description: Build the Add New Student form screen. Fields: Full Name, DOB (optional), Parent Name, Parent Email, Parent Phone, Package selector. Inline client-side validation. Submit calls POST /api/students.
Acceptance Criteria: Given the form is rendered; When mandatory fields are empty and Save is clicked; Then inline error messages appear. When all valid; Then success toast and redirect to student detail.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to edit an existing student's profile or deactivate a dropped-out student so that data remains accurate and inactive students are clearly marked.
Implement PUT endpoint for updating student fields and/or status (Active → Inactive). Must log change to Activity_Log.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT and valid student_id; When PUT called; Then record updated and Activity_Log entry created.

Description: Build the Student Detail screen with Edit Profile and Deactivate actions. Inline validation on editable fields.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a student is selected; When Edit Profile clicked; Then fields become editable. When Deactivate clicked; Then confirmation dialog shown.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to log a manual tuition fee payment against a student package so that the package balance (Remaining Sessions) is updated correctly.
Implement POST /api/payments. Validates Admin role (RBAC). Creates Payment row. Recalculates remaining_sessions on student_packages. Returns 201.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT and valid payload; When called; Then Payment row created and remaining_sessions updated.

Description: Build payment recording form. Fields: Student ID (dropdown), Amount, Payment Method (Cash/Bank Transfer), Payment Date. On success show MSG-03 toast.
Acceptance Criteria: Given the form is open; When valid data submitted; Then success toast displayed and payment appears in history.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to create session schedules by assigning a Class, Teacher, Room, Start Time and End Time so that the master timetable is defined and visible to Teachers.

Summary: API: POST /api/sessions — create teaching session with conflict check
Description: Implement POST /api/sessions. Run double-booking prevention algorithm (BR-03) before persisting. Returns 201 on success, 409 with conflict details on overlap.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a valid payload with no time overlap; When called; Then 201. Given an overlap; When called; Then 409 with conflict info.

Description: Build Create Session form. Dropdowns for Class, Teacher, Room. Date-time pickers for Start/End. On 409 redirect to conflict resolution screen.
Acceptance Criteria: Given valid non-conflicting inputs; When Save clicked; Then session created. Given conflict; Then conflict alert screen shown.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to see the details of a scheduling conflict (double-booked room or teacher) and choose an alternative resource so that the session can be saved without clashes.
Return available Teachers and Rooms for a given time slot, excluding already-booked ones.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a time range; When called; Then only free Teachers and Rooms returned.

Description: Display conflicting resource name (readonly). Show dropdown of available alternatives. On re-save, call POST /api/sessions again.
Acceptance Criteria: Given conflict screen is shown; When Admin selects an alternative and saves; Then session created if no new conflict.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to view a list of students whose package sessions are running low (≤ 2 weeks) so that I can contact parents and encourage renewal.

Summary: API: GET /api/alerts — list active renewal alerts
Description: Return all alert_notifications with status=Pending, type=Renewal, ordered by created_at.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When called; Then list of pending renewal alerts returned.

Summary: API: PATCH /api/alerts/{id} — mark alert as resolved
Description: Update alert_notification status to Resolved.
Acceptance Criteria: Given valid alert_id; When PATCH called; Then status updated to Resolved.

Description: Dashboard widget + full list view of renewal alerts. Show Student Name, Remaining Sessions, Parent Contact, Action Status. Button to mark Resolved.
Acceptance Criteria: Given alerts exist; When Admin opens list; Then all pending alerts displayed with action buttons.

Description: As a Teacher, I want to view my daily and weekly teaching schedule (Room, Time, Class) so that I can prepare for each session.
Return sessions for the authenticated Teacher filtered by date range (query params: start_date, end_date).
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher JWT; When called with date range; Then only that teacher's sessions returned.

Description: Build timetable view with Daily/Weekly toggle. Render session cards (Room, Time, Class name). Cards link to Class Session Details.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher is on My Timetable; When Daily/Weekly toggle selected; Then correct sessions displayed.

Description: As a Teacher, I want to mark each enrolled student as Present, Absent, or Late after a session and submit attendance so that records are saved and parents are notified automatically.
Accept attendance array (student_id, status, note). Persist records. Deduct 1 session from student_package per BR-04. Enqueue email notification payload.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher JWT and valid session_id; When POST called; Then attendance saved, sessions decremented, email queued.

Description: List enrolled students with radio buttons (Present/Absent/Late) and optional Note field. Submit button calls POST attendance API.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher opens attendance; When all statuses set and Submit clicked; Then confirmation shown.

Description: As a Teacher, I want to submit a performance score and feedback comment for each student within 12 hours of session completion (SLA) so that evaluations are timely and recorded.
Accept score (1–10), feedback_context. Validate SLA: if created_at > session_end + 12h, mark as late=true and log violation. Persist feedback.
Acceptance Criteria: Given within SLA; When POST called; Then feedback saved, late=false. Given past SLA; When POST called; Then feedback saved, late=true, violation logged.

Description: Fields: Score (1–10 slider/input), Feedback Context (textarea max 1000 chars), SLA Timer (live countdown). Submit button.
Acceptance Criteria: Given feedback screen open within SLA; When submitted; Then success. Given SLA expired; When submitted; Then MSG-02 warning shown.

Description: As the System, I want to run a nightly cron job at 00:00 that scans all active student packages and creates renewal alert records for those with ≤ 2 weeks of sessions remaining so that Admins are notified proactively.

Summary: TASK: Implement nightly depletion scan cron worker
Description: Write a scheduled background worker (e.g., pg_cron or Node cron). Query active student_packages. Compare remaining_sessions to threshold. Insert missing renewal alert_notifications.
Acceptance Criteria: Given midnight trigger; When worker runs; Then alert_notifications created for qualifying packages without duplicating existing alerts.

Summary: TASK: Configure cron schedule for depletion scan (00:00 daily)
Description: Set up infrastructure-level cron trigger (Supabase pg_cron or external scheduler) to invoke the depletion scan function at midnight.
Acceptance Criteria: Given production environment; When clock hits 00:00; Then worker is invoked automatically.

Description: As the System, I want to automatically send email notifications to parents via an Email Gateway (Attendance outcomes and Package renewal prompts) so that parents are informed without manual Admin effort.

Summary: TASK: Implement email notification queue processor
Description: Write queue consumer that picks pending alert_notifications, formats HTML template (Attendance / Renewal type), calls SMTP/Email API. Update status to Sent. Implement exponential retry (max 3) on failure.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a pending notification; When processor runs; Then email sent and status=Sent. On SMTP fail; Then retry up to 3x then status=Failed.

Description: Configure SMTP / API credentials for the chosen Email Gateway provider. Create email HTML templates (Attendance, Renewal). Test deliverability.
Acceptance Criteria: Given credentials configured; When test email triggered; Then email received in target inbox.

Description: As the System, I want strict RBAC enforced at API and UI layers (Admin vs Teacher roles) so that cross-domain data leaks are prevented (BR-05).
Acceptance Criteria:
Given an Admin JWT; When Teacher-only API called; Then 403 Forbidden.
Given a Teacher JWT; When Financial API called; Then 403 Forbidden.

Summary: NFR: Enforce HTTPS and Encrypted Transmission
Description: Ensure all API endpoints and the frontend web app are served exclusively over HTTPS (TLS). No HTTP fallback.
Acceptance Criteria: Given production deployment; When any HTTP request made; Then redirect to HTTPS or connection refused.

Summary: NFR: Teacher session token expiry after 2 hours inactivity
Description: Configure JWT or Supabase session TTL to expire Teacher tokens after 2 hours of inactivity.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher token issued; When 2h of inactivity; Then next API call returns 401.

Summary: NFR: Responsive UI — support minimum 360px viewport
Description: Ensure all frontend screens render correctly on viewports from 360px width upward (mobile, tablet, desktop).
Acceptance Criteria: Given the app is opened on a 360px device; When any core screen rendered; Then no horizontal scroll, all content usable.

Summary: NFR: UI Rendering Response < 1.5s on stable connection
Description: Ensure data retrieval actions (timetable load, student list) complete rendering within 1.5 seconds on a stable network.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a standard data load; When measured in browser DevTools; Then DOMContentLoaded + data render ≤ 1.5s.

Summary: NFR: Primary workflow completion in ≤ 3 clicks from Dashboard
Description: Audit navigation paths for recording attendance and renewing a package. Ensure each reachable in ≤ 3 clicks.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin or Teacher is on Dashboard; When navigating to attendance/payment; Then reached in 3 or fewer clicks.

Description: All system forms must include client-side inline validation displaying field-level error messages before API submission.
Acceptance Criteria: Given an invalid form field; When user attempts to submit; Then inline error shown without API call.

Description: Configure hosting (Supabase + frontend deployment) to target 99.9% uptime. Set up uptime monitoring alerts.
Acceptance Criteria: Given production deployment; When uptime monitoring runs; Then downtime alerts sent when service unavailable.

Summary: NFR: Email Gateway retry logic — max 3 retries on failure
Description: Implement exponential back-off retry (max 3 attempts) for failed SMTP/Email API calls before marking notification as Failed.
Acceptance Criteria: Given SMTP call fails; When retry logic runs; Then attempts up to 3 times with increasing delay before logging failure.

Summary: SETUP: Provision Supabase project (PostgreSQL DB + Auth + Storage)
Description: Create Supabase project. Configure PostgreSQL schema (all 14 tables per SDS 1.3). Enable Row Level Security. Configure Auth for Admin and Teacher roles.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Supabase project created; When schema migration runs; Then all 14 tables present with correct constraints.

Summary: SETUP: Define and run DB migration scripts (14 tables)
Description: Write SQL migration files for: users, activity_logs, classes, class_students, rooms, sessions, attendances, feedbacks, students, parents, packages, student_packages, payments, alert_notifications.
Acceptance Criteria: Given migration scripts applied; When DB schema checked; Then all tables exist with correct columns and FK constraints.

Description: Set up GitHub Actions workflows for: lint + test on PR, build and deploy on merge to main.
Acceptance Criteria: Given PR opened; When CI runs; Then lint and test pass. Given merge to main; When CD runs; Then app deployed.

Description: Connect frontend repo to Vercel/Netlify. Configure environment variables (SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY). Set up preview deployments for PRs.
Acceptance Criteria: Given frontend deployed; When URL accessed; Then app loads correctly with valid API connectivity.

Summary: SETUP: Environment configuration and secrets management
Description: Define .env structure. Store secrets (DB credentials, Email Gateway API keys) in CI/CD secrets and Supabase vault. Never commit secrets to repo.
Acceptance Criteria: Given secrets configured; When app starts; Then connects to DB and Email Gateway successfully without plaintext secrets in code.

Summary: TECH DEBT: Add integration test suite for API endpoints
Description: Write integration tests covering happy-path and error scenarios for all API endpoints identified in the SRS use cases.
Acceptance Criteria: Given test suite runs; When all UC endpoints exercised; Then all tests pass.

Summary: TECH DEBT: Add end-to-end UI smoke tests
Description: Implement Playwright or Cypress E2E smoke tests for primary user journeys: Add Student, Record Payment, Create Session, Submit Attendance, Submit Feedback.
Acceptance Criteria: Given E2E suite runs; When smoke tests executed; Then all journeys complete without error.

Description: As a system user (Admin or Teacher), I want to securely log in with my email and password and log out of the system so that my session is authenticated and protected.

Summary: API: POST /api/v1/auth/login — authenticate user & issue JWT
Description: Implement POST /api/v1/auth/login. Validates email/password against users table. Returns JWT with payload: user_id, role, email, fullName. Implement Supabase Auth or custom JWT signing.
Acceptance Criteria: Given valid email + password; When POST called; Then 200 with accessToken. Given wrong password; When called; Then 401. Given inactive user; When called; Then 403.

Summary: UI: Login Screen — Admin & Teacher portal entry point
Description: Build the Login screen as the entry point for both Admin and Teacher users. Fields: Email, Password. On success: redirect to respective Dashboard based on role. Show error message on failed login.
Acceptance Criteria: Given the login page; When correct credentials entered; Then redirect to Admin Dashboard (role=admin) or Teacher Dashboard (role=teacher). When wrong credentials; Then inline error shown.

Description: Implement logout endpoint. Invalidate the current JWT/session token server-side. Clear client-side token storage.
Acceptance Criteria: Given a valid JWT; When POST /logout called; Then 200 and token no longer accepted on subsequent calls.

Summary: SETUP: Seed master data scripts (packages, rooms, initial admin user)
Description: Write seed SQL/scripts to populate: packages catalogue (at least 3 packages), rooms list (at least 5 rooms), and 1 initial Admin user account for first login.
Acceptance Criteria: Given seed scripts run on fresh DB; When data queried; Then packages, rooms, and admin user exist with correct values.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to create, view, update, and deactivate Teacher accounts so that only authorised Teachers can log in and be assigned to sessions.
Implement: GET /api/v1/teachers (list), POST /api/v1/teachers (create), PUT /api/v1/teachers/{id} (update), PATCH /api/v1/teachers/{id}/status (activate/deactivate). All Admin-only (RBAC).
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET called; Then list of teachers returned. When POST with valid payload; Then teacher created. When status PATCH; Then active/inactive updated.

Description: Build Teacher list screen with Add Teacher button, search/filter, and per-row Activate/Deactivate toggle. Add Teacher form: Full Name, Email, Password.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin on Teacher list; When Add clicked; Then form modal opens. When saved; Then teacher appears in list.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to create, view, and update class groups so that students can be enrolled into classes and sessions can be assigned against them.
Acceptance Criteria:
Given Admin creates a Class with Class Name and Level;
Then the class is saved and available for Session scheduling (UC-04) and Student enrollment (UC-01).

Description: Implement: GET /api/v1/classes (list), POST /api/v1/classes (create), PUT /api/v1/classes/{id} (update). Admin-only.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET called; Then all classes returned. When POST with name; Then class created.

Description: Build Class list with Add Class form. Minimum fields: Class Name, Level/Grade. Class list used as dropdown in Create Session screen (UC-04).
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin on Class list; When Add clicked and saved; Then class appears in list and in UC-04 dropdown.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to create and manage physical classroom rooms so that they can be assigned to scheduled sessions without conflicts.
Acceptance Criteria:
Given Admin creates a Room with Room Name and Capacity;
Then the room is available for selection in Create Session (UC-04).

Description: Implement: GET /api/v1/rooms (list), POST /api/v1/rooms (create), PUT /api/v1/rooms/{id} (update). Admin-only.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET called; Then all rooms returned. When POST; Then room created and available for scheduling.

Description: Build Room list with Add Room form. Fields: Room Name, Capacity. Room list used as dropdown in Create Session (UC-04).
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin on Room list; When Add clicked and saved; Then room appears in list and in UC-04 dropdown.

Description: As an Academic Admin, I want to define the catalogue of study packages (session count, duration, price) so that packages can be assigned to students upon enrollment.
Acceptance Criteria:
Given Admin creates a Package with Name, Session Count, Duration (weeks), Price;
Then it appears in the package dropdown when onboarding a student (UC-01) and recording payments (UC-03).

Description: Implement: GET /api/v1/packages (list), POST /api/v1/packages (create), PUT /api/v1/packages/{id} (update). Admin-only.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET called; Then all packages returned. When POST; Then package created and available for student assignment.

Description: Build Package list with Add Package form. Fields: Package Name, Session Count, Duration, Price (VND). Packages are used in UC-01 (onboarding) and UC-03 (payment).
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin on Package list; When Add clicked and saved; Then package appears in list and in enrollment dropdowns.

Summary: UI: Admin Dashboard Screen — overview & navigation hub
Description: Build the Admin Dashboard as the main entry point after login. Widgets: Active Students count, Pending Renewal Alerts count, Today's Sessions count, Recent Payments. Quick navigation to all modules.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin logs in; When Dashboard loads; Then summary widgets display current counts. When widget clicked; Then redirects to respective module list.

Description: Build the Teacher home screen after login. Show today's session schedule as the primary content. Quick links to Timetable and Attendance recording.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher logs in; When Dashboard loads; Then today's sessions shown. When session card clicked; Then Class Session Detail opens.

Description: Implement GET /api/v1/student-profiles with query params: search (name), status (Active/Inactive), page, limit. Returns paginated list of student profiles.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET with no params; Then all students returned paginated. When search param set; Then filtered results returned.

Description: Build Student List screen. Show table: Name, Status, Package, Remaining Sessions. Search bar by name. Status filter (Active/Inactive). Row click navigates to Student Profile Detail (UC-02).
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin on Student List; When page loads; Then paginated student list shown. When search typed; Then filtered in real-time. When row clicked; Then Student Detail screen opens.

Description: Implement GET /api/v1/tuition-payments with optional filter: studentId, dateFrom, dateTo. Returns list of payment records with amount, method, date, student name.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET called; Then paginated list returned. When studentId filter applied; Then only that student's payments shown.

Description: Build Payment History table screen accessible from Admin Dashboard or Student Detail. Columns: Student Name, Amount (VND), Payment Method, Date. Export to CSV optional.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin opens Payment History; When loaded; Then all transactions listed newest first. When filter by student; Then filtered.

Summary: API: GET /api/v1/teaching-schedules — list all sessions (Admin view)
Description: Implement GET /api/v1/teaching-schedules with filters: classId, teacherId, dateFrom, dateTo. Returns full session list for Admin master timetable view.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET called; Then all sessions returned. When date range filter applied; Then filtered sessions returned.

Summary: UI: Master Schedule / Session List Screen (Admin)
Description: Build Admin-facing master timetable view. Calendar or list view of all sessions with Class, Teacher, Room, Time. Filter by date range, class, teacher. Row click opens Session Detail with Edit/Delete options.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin on Master Schedule; When loaded; Then all sessions visible in grid/list. When filter applied; Then scoped view shown.

Description: Implement soft-delete or status update for sessions (status: Cancelled). Admin-only. Must validate no attendance records have been submitted for this session.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT and valid session_id with no attendance; When DELETE called; Then session marked Cancelled. Given session has attendance; When DELETE called; Then 409 with explanation.

Description: Implement GET /api/v1/academic-feedback with filters: sessionId, studentId, isLate, dateFrom, dateTo. Returns all feedback records including SLA violation flags.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin JWT; When GET called; Then all feedback records returned. When isLate=true filter; Then only SLA-breached submissions shown.

Description: Build Admin-facing Feedback List screen. Table: Teacher, Class, Student, Score, Date Submitted, SLA Status (On-time / Late). Filter by SLA status to audit violations.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Admin opens Feedback Management; When loaded; Then all submitted feedback listed. When SLA filter = Late; Then only MSG-02 flagged items shown.

Description: Implement GET /api/v1/class-attendances with sessionId query param. Returns list of attendance records (student, status, note) for a given session.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher or Admin JWT; When GET with valid sessionId; Then all attendance records for that session returned.

Summary: UI: Class Session Detail / Attendance History Screen
Description: Extend Class Session Detail to show submitted attendance records in a read-only table. Accessible from Teacher Timetable card click (UC-07). Show: Student Name, Status (Present/Absent/Late), Note.
Acceptance Criteria: Given Teacher clicks a session card on Timetable; When Session Detail opens; Then if attendance already submitted: show read-only records. If not submitted: show attendance form (UC-08).

Summary: NFR: Set up application error logging (e.g., Sentry)
Description: Integrate error monitoring (Sentry or equivalent). Capture unhandled exceptions on both frontend and backend. Configure alert notifications to team channel.
Acceptance Criteria: Given an unhandled exception occurs; When it happens in production; Then it is captured in Sentry with stack trace and alert sent.

Summary: NFR: Set up uptime monitoring (e.g., UptimeRobot)
Description: Configure external uptime monitor to ping the deployed app every 5 minutes. Alert via email/Slack if downtime detected. Supports 99.9% SLA target.
Acceptance Criteria: Given app is deployed; When uptime monitor is configured; Then ping results logged. When app goes down; Then alert sent within 5 minutes.

Summary: NFR: Cron job execution logging & failure alerting
Description: Add structured logging to UC-10 depletion scan cron worker: log start time, records scanned, alerts created, end time, errors. Alert DevOps if job fails to complete within 3-minute window.
Acceptance Criteria: Given cron runs at 00:00; When job completes; Then execution log written with metrics. When job fails or times out; Then alert sent.

Summary: TECH DEBT: Unit tests for BLL (Business Logic Layer) services
Description: Write unit tests for all BLL service classes (StudentService, PaymentService, ScheduleService, AlertService, AttendanceService, FeedbackService, AutomationService). Mock DAL dependencies.
Acceptance Criteria: Given unit test suite runs; When services tested with valid and invalid inputs; Then all business rule validations (BR-01 to BR-05) are verified by tests.

Summary: TECH DEBT: Code linting and formatting rules (ESLint + Prettier)
Description: Set up ESLint and Prettier configs for the frontend and backend codebase. Add lint check as a CI gate on every PR (GitHub Actions).
Acceptance Criteria: Given a PR is opened with lint violations; When CI runs; Then lint check fails and PR is blocked. Given properly formatted code; When CI runs; Then lint check passes.
